description: §38.11 — a `<channel name="chat-${@room}">` whose `name=` string-literal carries a `${...}` marker fires E-CHANNEL-007. `name=` is a static-literal-only attribute (§38.3): the `${...}` survives codegen as a literal substring of the WS URL, silently collapsing every intended per-room channel onto one topic (F-CHANNEL-001).
rationale: §38.11 / §38.3: the `name=` (and `topic=`) attribute value SHALL be a static string literal — no `${...}` interpolation. A `name=` literal containing `${` SHALL emit E-CHANNEL-007 (VP-3). For per-instance scoping, use a static name + payload-side filtering.
